opp.name: Bandhan Bank_S2P
opp.next_step: 75% Confidence: avoma update- https://app.avoma.com/meetings/cf74f134-1d88-42c6-a804-7b63124918fb 2nd Jul- Nishan revealed we are coming 1st on product assessment- with only blocker the Zycus FSI experience in India- Carl to meet CFO & CIo for final close presentation week of 13th Jul. (we need a partner to provide experienced India FSI project experience in final meeting) 29th June: We had good overall feedback and only negative points for no India BFSI implementation. Today they are going to have this discussion with the CFO on the next steps, there can be 2 outcomes 1. They directly ask for commercials and negotiate with top3 vendors 2. Their CFO might call us for a demo , majorly from the angle of implementation.. here we can take share our plan for taking help from our partners having India BFSI implementation. 23rd June : Had a conversation with My Champion (Nishan), asking for the next steps and commercial discussion, he asked that we will get the details and commercial template over the email soon. In parallel I am inviting Satish the head of Accounts and finance operation to Horizon. 17th June: Had a presentation and demo with the entire evaluation team of Bandhan Bank. all the key stake holders were very impressed and took more interest in Zycus personally. Red Flag - We don't have banking implementation in India. Next steps- After 2 weeks they will setup a call with Me and Carl for introducing us to their CFO/ and management. Also in parallel they will request for the commercials. In one on one discussion Head of Accounts and finance operations asked us to focus more on the commercials 8th June : Final Presentation and demo in Kolkata 17th June with their management 28th May- 2nd June presentation/ Demo moved to 2nd week of June as Bandhan Bank had their emergency meeting 20th May - Demo is postponed to 1st week of June as they got some internal meetings 19th May: Confirmed date for final demo -22nd May 15th May- Dilip from Bandhan updated on the evaluation process, we should get the demo invite within this week 13th May- NOTES added with update 7th May: Shared the Feasibility document and checked on the status - they will get back on the dates as their CFO is travelling, mostly it should be next week, no evaluation started for any other vendor. 6th May: Feasibility documents needs to be submitted and checking with the Bandhan team on the confirmed dates. 28th April: We got the invitation from Dilip (Bandhan Bank) for presenting to their management around 10th May. Meeting will focus on Zycus differentiation and platform Demo. 23rd April - I have shared them the Zycus differentiation for Bandhan bankbased on their pain points. 17th April - We had a joint meeting with their entire team from payment, procurement and supplier team - this was information gathering call. We captured their pain point. Confidence 50% 9th April'26 - Spoke to nishan from Bandhan bank - He mentioned we have been shortlisted to top 6 vendors next round and to proceed with next round details to be given he asked for a quick 15 minutes connect today ie.9th april'26 - 5pm . asking akshay and carl for availability 16th March'26- Dilip from Bandhan bank has queries on ourP2P proposal and wants to discuss it. Meeting scheduled for 16th mar-2.30 pm proposal queries discussion. MEETING DONE - Email with collaterals shared to review our offering. 27th Feb'26 - Spoke to Mrinmoy over Indian number - He said they are evaluating and assesing the proposals. Will take another week and he will revert he said. 24th Feb'25 - Spoke to Saipayan - He said its still under technical evaluation with mrinmoy yet - check with him 17th Feb'26 - Spoke to Mrinmoy Mahapatra from Bandhan Bank - And told that we have filled the responses to the RFP shared by them. and as clarified them to ask if they have any further queries about it and told we have strong intent to be taking this ahead and getting shortlisted. 16th Feb'26 - Spoke to Saipayan from Bandhan - He clearly stated that they cant extend the further date of response submission to RFP + he stated the repsonse submission doesnt have a fixed format - we can submit in our format or template catering to the requirements and asks mentioned in the RFP ( techno-functional ask in RFP) - He said that needs to be sent to the person mentioned in the rfp - who will further evaluate all vendors and their responses and then share it with procrement team - then the commercials are brought to picture and collected - then they will be rating the Vendors L1,L2 etc and then shortlist them based on the highest feasible -- May ask for demo between these stages after technical evalution or even after the commercial evalution depends on the team. Then will go submit and finalize the bid. This is what he said. Now - he aked us to submit the response by 17th EOD - no extension and submit in our format. 11th Feb'26 - RFP Response date extended to 17th Feb email received. -- Shared this rfp and extension internally to Carl and Akshay to guide next steps. 5th Feb 2026| Ria - RFP RECEIVED 10% confidence 12th Feb 2026- Prospect has come back with a "late to Zycus" RFP- suspiciously looks like we are being added as a column B in any evaluation now but given the logo here, we should re-qualify via BD- BANT and more, to progress this one 16/6: Prospect has no budget approval to run this project. 7/2/2025: Completed steps - Intro call, Solution overview with pre sales team, QC Call with Madhuri and Carl Next Steps: Schedule and Conduct a Demo Meeting: Action: Organize a demo meeting to present the Zycus solution to Bandhan Bank. Objective: Showcase the automated vendor selection, performance tracking features, and how the solution meets regulatory and scalability needs. Develop Comprehensive Presentation Materials: Action: Create a detailed presentation integrating: The 5-minute highlight video (created by Carl) emphasizing the solution’s key functionalities. High-level pricing estimates and the detailed feature comparison provided by Madhuri. Supporting documents and charts illustrating the benefits of a centralized, automated procurement process. Follow-Up Communication: Action: Use the established email chain (managed by Richa) to address any further topics or questions raised by Mr. Aratla Pavani. Objective: Ensure clear, concise, and timely communication to reinforce the value proposition of our solution and prepare for the next phase of engagement.
opp.ais_why:
• Across 15+ months of dated rep notes covering RFP receipt, response, shortlisting and management presentation, there is zero buyer-side mention of AI, Merlin, agentic, autonomous, or any AI capability. The 17 Feb 2026 rep note records: 'Spoke to Mrinmoy Mahapatra from Bandhan Bank... told that we have filled the responses to the RFP shared by them... and clarified them to ask if they have any further queries about it.' The dialogue is entirely RFP-procedural, not AI-themed.
• On 9 Apr 2026 nishan.konar @bandhanbank.com confirmed: 'we have been shortlisted to top 6 vendors next round.' On 28 Apr 2026: 'We got the invitation from Dilip (Bandhan Bank) for presenting to their management around 10th May. Meeting will focus on Zycus differentiation and platform Demo.' Even the management pitch is framed as 'Zycus differentiation and platform Demo' ‚Äî no AI use cases, no Merlin demo focus, no agentic conversation despite Merlin being in product scope.
• 9 Avoma meetings with verified @bandhanbank.com attendees corroborate the engagement is real but generic. Confidence is High because the NextStep is unusually rich (4,381 chars across 15+ touchpoints) and the absence of any AI keyword across that volume is itself strong evidence. Merlin appears in Products but never in conversation; this is a traditional BFSI P2P RFP, not an AI-driven evaluation.

Prospect has no budget approval to run this project.
this was the only red flag... you have no experience in BFSI clients for India.
keep a rough estimate... for a CFO that's a big deal to find out what is the cost.

metrics.narrative: The business problem is heavily manual, decentralized procure-to-pay across IT and non-IT procurement plus payments; Nishan framed the mandate on 17 Apr as 'maximum automation... minimize manual work... that is the north star of this product.' The only volume figure surfaced is non-IT spend of 'roughly around 64 per annum' (Nirmal, 17 Apr); no savings target, headcount reduction, cycle-time, or ROI percentage has been quantified by the buyer. On 02 Jul Nishan warned the CFO will ask for 'ballpark figures on the costs of the whole thing,' so a value case is now needed but not built. Move: quantify automation savings, headcount optimization, and payment-status/GRN error reduction to anchor price for the CFO session.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Nishan Kumar, DVP - Strategy & Program Management
champion.narrative: Nishan Kumar (DVP - Strategy & Program Management) is a strong, active champion with CFO access who orchestrates the deal internally. He proactively shares insider intel (02 Jul: 'you are coming 1st on product assessment'), coaches Zycus on exactly how to pass the CFO gate (agenda, ballpark cost, the external-consultant fix), arranged the full evaluation-team demo on 17 Jun, and is setting up the CFO/CIO final meeting. Last contact 02 Jul (one day before this sweep); no cooling signals. He is the reason Zycus was given a 'second chance' after the implementation red flag.

competition.narrative: This is a 7-vendor RFP now down to a top 3, with Zycus scored #1 on both product and technology; Nishan called Zycus 'the surprise package' that beat expected big names like Deloitte (02 Jul). The named rivals on record are Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER and SAP Ariba. No single competitor is buyer-preferred; the real competitive threat is implementation credibility, where a rival differentiated by bringing an ex-IDFC-bank consultant onto its proposal (Nishan, 02 Jul). Wedge: Zycus wins on product and technology plus Merlin AI automation, so the path is to neutralize the India BFSI implementation gap with a named local delivery partner before the CFO meeting.

sources: 17 Apr 2026 P2P Meeting (Anmol, Mrinmoy Mahapatra, Arun)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed and specific: 'the entire process is manual,' managed over email and phone with data held in Excel (Anmol, 17 Apr); a legacy Java, form-based ERP with poor user experience (Mrinmoy, 17 Apr); no maker-checker on GRN so 'a user may wrongly cancel a GRN without understanding the financial impact' (Arun, 17 Apr); multi-point re-entry of data from RFP to vendor creation to payment; and vendors chasing payment status manually (Mrinmoy, 17 Apr). Pain owners are Mrinmoy Mahapatra (IT), Nirmal (non-IT procurement) and the payments team (Arun/Dilip). Confirmed across the evaluation team.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are known at a high level: after purchase-committee approval by threshold, a PO/POA and contract are issued while the MSA signing runs in parallel, with vendor onboarding via the eGL/EGM master and a TPRM questionnaire (Anmol, 17 Apr). Third-party/supplier risk and MSA governance are handled separately by the vendor-management team on ServiceNow (Nirmal, 17 Apr). No India BFSI implementation partner or SOW has been formalized yet, and InfoSec/integration deep-dive with the relevant stakeholder (their ERP) is still ahead. Move: scope the delivery-partner arrangement and confirm the MSA/purchase-committee path in the CFO session.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Sayantan Mukherjee, Branch Credit Manager
economic_buyer.narrative: The final decision sits with the CFO (named by the champion as Rajiv Mantry) together with the f&a head and CIO; Nishan stated on 17 Jun that 'your final evaluation will be done with the top management, our CFO... and the CIO.' Zycus has never met the CFO or CIO; the first exposure is the final presentation scheduled for the week of 13 Jul, which Carl Kimball (VP APJ) will lead. A material risk flag exists: the 16 Jun Next_Step records 'Prospect has no budget approval to run this project,' so budget authority is unconfirmed. Sayantan Mukherjee (Branch Credit Manager) is referenced on the 17 Jun demo as the CPO. Move: use the 13 Jul session to open a direct CFO relationship and confirm budget approval.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a structured multi-stage RFP: requirement/RFP publish to 3-5 vendors, independent techno-functional scoring by committees, then only qualified vendors submit commercials, followed by multi-round negotiation with all qualified vendors, purchase-committee approval by threshold value, then PO and MSA issued in parallel (Anmol, 17 Apr). The field is now down to a top 3 (29 Jun Next_Step), the full evaluation-team demo happened 17 Jun, and seniority is rising: the CFO, f&a head and CIO enter for the final decision meeting around 13 Jul. This is escalation into commercial and executive gates, not an early-stage process.

decision_criteria.narrative: Nishan named the three scoring criteria explicitly on 02 Jul: 'there were 3 criteria, product, implementation, and technology... Product, you are number one. Even technology, you are number one. It is in that implementation where you got lower scores.' The formal process is a techno-functional evaluation scored independently by multiple technical and business evaluators, averaged out of 10, before qualified vendors submit commercials (Anmol, 17 Apr; Saipayan, 16 Feb Next_Step). India BFSI implementation credibility is the single decisive gap; product and technology are already won. Confirmed and well-evidenced.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're well-positioned — the real risk is timing and sign-off, not a competitor.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Win signals and momentum lift the underlying read to ~75 — but we cap confidence at 70 until the stage advances and sign-off lands. Read the gap as 'earned, but not yet banked.'
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — Nishan Kumar (DVP - Strategy & Program Management) is a strong, active champion with CFO access. He supplies real-time scoring intel, coaches Zycus on the CFO agenda and the implementation fix, arranged the full evaluation-team demo, and secured the CFO/CIO final meeting. Last contact 02 Jul, no cooling signals. Risk is single-threading: he is currently the only route to power.
bullets.text: Champion is driving it — Nishan Kumar (DVP - Strategy & Program Management) is a strong, active champion with CFO access.
Live next steps with named dates

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Seven-vendor BFSI RFP down to a top 3, with Zycus scored #1 on product and #1 on technology as of 02 Jul (champion Nishan) and named the 'surprise package' over expected big brands like Deloitte.

bullets.text: No India BFSI implementation experience; the single red flag scoring Zycus low, a rival is fielding an ex-IDFC-bank consultant
CFO and CIO (final economic buyers) not yet engaged; first meeting only in week of 13 Jul, after the close date
Budget approval to run the project is not in place; and L1/commercial pressure across the top-3 field
Close date 08 Jul is not achievable; realistic close ~Sep 2026 after CFO meeting, commercials, negotiation and contracting

items: Secure a named India-banking delivery partner or consultant for the CFO meeting. (by 10 Jul)
Build the CFO/CIO business deck with implementation plan and ballpark cost estimate. (by 11 Jul)
Deliver the CFO and CIO final presentation in person and open a direct EB relationship. (by 17 Jul)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

contributions.evidence: Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 21 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 70 is carried by momentum 73 vs stage-expected 56; preference strength +1.00 (weight 20). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 73 (moving forward): seniority rising (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 54 is anchored on the stage win (70), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 73, and dragged by risk 21. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).
Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.
Next Step carries 2 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 3.0 / Negative reflects the RFP-procedural tone of most touchpoints, but the calls show latent management appetite: Nishan flagged AI/automation as a key final-evaluation focus (17 Apr) and told Zycus to 'keep something for an AI part, that will be your big selling point' (02 Jul). Field reads resistant; call evidence shows curiosity at the CFO/CIO tier that is currently under-served.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain around fully manual P2P and non-IT spend of ~64 per annum stated, but no ROI, savings, or payback quantified; CFO will ask for ballpark cost.

items.change: Full evaluation-team demo delivered in Kolkata; all key stakeholders impressed, single negative was no India BFSI implementation
Management feedback positive; buyer to decide between direct top-3 commercials or a CFO implementation demo
Champion confirmed Zycus ranked #1 of 7 vendors on product and technology; CFO/CIO final presentation secured for week of 13 Jul

deal_movement.summary: Deal is LIVE and accelerating: full evaluation-team demo 17 Jun, positive management feedback 29 Jun, and champion confirmation on 02 Jul that Zycus ranks #1 of 7 on product and technology with a CFO/CIO final meeting secured for the week of 13 Jul. Last real buyer touch 02 Jul.

reasons.detail: On the 02 Jul call champion Nishan confirmed Zycus scored #1 on both product and technology and was the 'surprise package' over Deloitte, but the sole remaining blocker is Zycus's India BFSI implementation experience. A shortlisted competitor is actively exploiting this gap by fielding an ex-IDFC-bank delivery consultant into the evaluation. With the CFO/CIO final close the week of 13 Jul and Zycus yet to lock a named India-banking delivery partner, this single credibility flag could flip a deal we otherwise lead - forfeiting a $150K forecasted BFSI logo.
reasons.metric: #1 on product & tech, yet India FSI experience is the sole blocker (02 Jul)
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Tanmay whether we have locked a named India-banking delivery partner for the 13 Jul CFO meeting to neutralize the ex-IDFC consultant the rival is fielding.
reasons.summary: Rival out-credits us on India banking delivery via an ex-IDFC-bank implementation consultant
reasons.evidence: Competitive position (02 Jul): 'the strongest CURRENT threat is ... the India BFSI implementation-credibility gap, which a shortlisted competitor is exploiting by fielding an ex-IDFC-bank delivery consultant'; champion Nishan named the only blocker as 'the Zycus FSI experience in India'.

critical_signals.text: At the 17 Jun management demo Zycus was scored the number-one vendor of seven on both product and technology and called the surprise package.
The sole blocker is a lack of India banking implementation experience, and a shortlisted rival is exploiting it with an ex-IDFC-bank delivery consultant.
The CFO and CIO enter for the final decision meeting around 13 July, and Zycus has never met either of them.
Pricing has not been exchanged and budget approval is unconfirmed, yet the CFO will expect a ballpark implementation cost.
The buyer now expects a named India-banking implementation partner and proven Oracle integration for the executive session.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and strengthening: strong demo reception, champion active one day before sweep, and seniority rising into the decision. Not cooling.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 10 Feb 2025, qualified 17 Feb 2026, close 08 Jul 2026 = ~513-day enterprise BFSI cycle. In Shortlisted stage since ~02 Jul (down-select from 7 vendors). From 03 Jul the remaining path is: CFO/CIO final presentation week of 13 Jul (10+ days out, AFTER close), commercial template exchange (promised 23 Jun, not received), pricing and top-3 negotiation, purchase-committee approval by threshold, MSA/InfoSec, contracting. Minimum realistic runway 8-10 weeks = Sep 2026. Pace required to hit 08 Jul: not achievable. Forward slip 60-75 days.
evidence: 02 Jul call, Nishan Kumar: 'it was probably the best presentation of all the 7 vendors... Product wise, you got number one. Even technology, you are number one. It is in that implementation where you got lower scores.'
02 Jul call, Nishan Kumar: 'we decided you'll get another chance with our CFO... It will be probably 10 days from now.'
29 Jun Next_Step: 'good overall feedback and only negative points for no India BFSI implementation... 1. directly ask for commercials and negotiate with top3 vendors 2. CFO might call us for a demo.'
23 Jun Next_Step: 'asking for the next steps and commercial discussion, he asked that we will get the details and commercial template over the email soon.'
16 Jun Next_Step: 'Prospect has no budget approval to run this project.'
north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy, live deal ranked #1 of 7 on product and technology, but the 08 Jul close is impossible: the CFO/CIO final presentation is only scheduled for the week of 13 Jul (after close), commercials are not yet exchanged, and top-3 negotiation plus purchase-committee approval still lie ahead. The date will slip to roughly Sep 2026; the deal itself is on a positive trajectory.

flags: Close the sole blocker: bring a named India-banking (ex-IDFC/Axis-type) delivery partner to the 13 Jul CFO session.
Convert champion intel into direct power access: use the CFO/CIO meeting to open a real EB relationship and confirm budget approval.
Build a quantified ROI and ballpark-cost story now to defend price against L1 competition in the top-3 negotiation.
best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy levers for a healthy top-3 deal whose only real risk is implementation credibility and an unengaged economic buyer.

customer_preference.evidence: Nishan, 02 Jul: 'the best presentation of all the 7 vendors... Product wise, you got number one. Even technology, you are number one'; buyer gave Zycus a second chance with the CFO.

competitive_position.summary: Seven-vendor BFSI RFP down to a top 3, with Zycus scored #1 on product and #1 on technology as of 02 Jul (champion Nishan) and named the 'surprise package' over expected big brands like Deloitte. No single rival is buyer-preferred; the strongest CURRENT threat is not a specific logo but the India BFSI implementation-credibility gap, which a shortlisted competitor is exploiting by fielding an ex-IDFC-bank delivery consultant (02 Jul). Among the SF-listed field, GEP Worldwide is the closest size/fit peer. How we win: bring a named India-banking delivery partner to the CFO meeting to close the only red flag, then defend price with a quantified ROI case in the top-3 negotiation.

17th Feb'26 - Spoke to Mrinmoy Mahapatra from Bandhan Bank - And told that we have filled the responses to the RFP shared by them. and as clarified them to ask if they have any further queries about it and told we have strong intent to be taking this ahead and getting shortlisted. 

 

16th Feb'26 - Spoke to Saipayan from Bandhan - He clearly stated that they cant extend the further date of response submission to RFP + he stated the repsonse submission doesnt have a fixed format - we can submit in our format or template catering to the requirements and asks mentioned in the RFP ( techno-functional ask in RFP) -

He said that needs to be sent to the person mentioned in the rfp - who will further evaluate all vendors and their responses and then share it with procrement team - then the commercials are brought to picture and collected - then they will be rating the Vendors L1,L2 etc and then shortlist them based on the highest feasible -- May ask for demo between these stages after technical evalution or even after the commercial evalution depends on the team.

Then will go submit and finalize the bid. This is what he said.

Now - he aked us to submit the response by 17th EOD - no extension and submit in our format.
